response.justification: Wei Chen has strong senior software engineering fundamentals and 19+ years of experience across systems and performance optimization, but their background is entirely in software development (web, backend, infrastructure). This role requires principal-level expertise in hardware architecture, SoC/IP modeling, and AI accelerator optimization—areas not represented in the resume. The candidate would need to learn chip design, deep learning, and hardware optimization essentially from scratch, making this a poor fit for a senior IC position.
strengths: Senior-level software architecture and systems design demonstrated across 19 years including distributed systems handling millions of requests/second
Cross-domain experience bridging hardware and software at National Instruments and Altom, including work with embedded systems and real-time constraints
Strong performance optimization background at scale (low-latency systems at Dunnhumby, microservices infrastructure, real-time metadata processing)
concerns: Zero experience with hardware design, chip architecture, SoC modeling, or IP integration—core technical requirements for the role
No background in AI/deep learning or neural network optimization, which is central to analyzing and optimizing AI accelerator workloads
Career focused exclusively on software development and infrastructure; lacks domain expertise in hardware-software co-design at the silicon level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design experience
Performance optimization experience in software domain
